people.name: Aighetta Quartet
people.bio: French guitar quartet. Lineup has changed over the years (members have included Alexandre Del Fa, François Szönyi, Olivier Fautrat, Pascal Rabatti, and Philippe Loli); exact lineup on the 2003 recording below is unconfirmed.

people.name: Alain Pérez
people.bio: Bass.

people.name: Ali Jackson
people.bio: Drummer, longtime member of the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra.

people.name: Ángela Bautista
people.bio: Palmas, chorus.

people.name: Anthony Newman
people.bio: American organist, harpsichordist, and conductor; a Bach specialist and frequent Marsalis collaborator (In Gabriel's Garden).

people.name: Antonio el Negro
people.bio: Palmas, chorus.

people.name: Ben Bernie, Maceo Pinkard, Kenneth Casey

people.bio: Flamenco cantaor (singer) discovered by Chano Domínguez.

people.bio: Bob Mintzer

people.name: Carles Benavent
people.bio: Also plays mandolin.

people.name: Carlos Henriquez
people.bio: Bassist, longtime member of the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra.

people.bio: Spanish pianist from Cádiz who fuses flamenco with jazz; frequent collaborator with Wynton Marsalis and the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra.

people.name: Chris Crenshaw
people.bio: Trombonist, vocalist, and composer/arranger with the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra.

people.name: Christian Garros
people.bio: French jazz drummer; the Jacques Loussier Play Bach trio.

people.bio: Kenny Barron, Stefon Harris, Ron Carter, Lewis Nash. *Plays Bach* (2002).

people.name: Donald Hunsberger
people.bio: American conductor, longtime music director of the Eastman Wind Ensemble (1965–2001) and an arranger of orchestral works for wind band.

people.name: Duquende
people.bio: Vocals.

people.name: Eddie Higgins
people.bio: Eddie Higgins

people.name: Eddie South
people.bio: Classically trained jazz violinist. Recorded Bach's Double Violin Concerto with Grappelli and Django (Paris, 1937).

people.bio: Slovak coloratura soprano celebrated for her bel canto roles.

people.bio: Opera bass-baritone; featured on Chick Corea's 'Spanish Suite'.

people.name: Esa-Pekka Salonen
people.bio: Finnish conductor and composer, former music director of the Los Angeles Philharmonic.

people.name: Flora Purim
people.bio: Brazilian jazz vocalist; sang with Return to Forever.

people.bio: Recorded Spain / Concierto de Aranjuez with Chick Corea (*Rendezvous in New York*, 2003).

people.name: Guadiana
people.bio: Palmas, chorus.

people.name: Guy Pedersen
people.bio: French jazz bassist; rhythm section on the Swingle Singers' Bach recordings.

people.name: Hiromi
people.bio: Recorded Spain / Concierto de Aranjuez as a piano duet with Chick Corea (*Duet*, 2009).

people.name: I Pommeriggi Musicali di Milano
people.bio: Milan chamber orchestra (I Pomeriggi Musicali, founded 1945). Plays the three-part orchestral title suite on John McLaughlin's [[Thieves & Poets]].

people.name: Israel Suarez (El Piraña)
people.bio: Madrid-born cajón virtuoso; longtime member of Paco de Lucía's band.

people.name: Jacques Loussier
people.bio: French pianist; founder of the Play Bach trio.

people.name: Jacques Loussier Trio
people.bio: French pianist Jacques Loussier's trio; the *Play Bach* series (from 1959) set Bach to swing.

people.name: Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra
people.bio: The resident big band of Jazz at Lincoln Center, led by Wynton Marsalis.

people.name: Jimmy Forrest / Oscar Washington / Lewis C Simpkins
Joe Farrell
people.bio: Jazz saxophonist and flutist; Return to Forever.

people.name: Joe Temperley
people.bio: Scottish-born baritone saxophonist, longtime member of the Jazz at Lincoln Center Orchestra.

people.name: John Nelson
people.bio: American conductor best known for his Berlioz interpretations; led the Indianapolis Symphony and later the Ensemble Orchestral de Paris.
